body.title: /debug skill: pointer resolution → Scraps → Haiku → Sonnet → report
body.result: /debug skill written: pointer resolution, Scraps extraction, Haiku/Sonnet analysis, structured report format. Skill at ~/.claude/skills/debug/SKILL.md + canonical copy.
body.description:
Implement the /debug CC skill — autonomous mixed-mode debugger that returns a structured report so CC diagnoses without interactive investigation.

Flow: (1) Pointer resolution (light inference): parse freeform input (ticket ID, timestamp like '160401', pasted console text, error snippet) into a structured log query {component, ts_start, ts_end, ticket_id?}; (2) Call T-scraps-debug-extractor to extract log window, state, test output deterministically; (3) Haiku: classify failure type, generate HYPOTHESIS with confidence score (0.0-1.0), suggest NEXT step; (4) Escalate to Sonnet if Haiku confidence < 0.5; (5) Format and return structured report.

Report format:
```
DEBUG REPORT — <timestamp>
Component: <X>  Ticket: <T-xxx>  Phase: <Y>

SUMMARY: <one line>

WHERE:    <file>:<line>  fn=<function>  (<context>)
WHAT:     <expected> vs <actual>

STATE:    <type-specific fields>

LOG WINDOW (<ts_start> → <ts_end>):
  <pre-extracted lines>

HYPOTHESIS (<confidence>): <diagnosis>
  check: <specific command to verify>

NEXT: <action>
```

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/debug/ (new skill file)
**Design rules:** pointer resolution uses minimal inference (one small call); Scraps and Haiku do the heavy lifting; escalate to Sonnet only on low confidence; report is the deliverable — CC reads it, doesn't investigate
**Scope boundary:** IN — skill file, pointer resolution, report formatting; OUT — Scraps extraction logic (T-scraps-debug-extractor)
**Test plan:** /debug with a known ticket ID produces a report with correct SUMMARY and WHERE; /debug with pasted timestamp text resolves to correct log window; report produced without CC needing follow-up commands
